Record Class EdnaOptions

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Record
de.kleinert.edna.EdnaOptions
Record Components:
allowSchemeUTF32Codes - Allows chars of the form \\xXXXXXXXX where uppercase X represents any hex digit. The value is limited to #\x0000FFFF (@link Character#MAX_VALUE).
allowDispatchChars - Allows characters to start with the dispatch sign #. These characters are then treated as instances of Char32 and therefore limited to 0..Char32.MAX_VALUE. This also allows the use of Unicode literals with eight hexadecimal digits of the form #\\uXXXXXXXX. The option is compatible with allowSchemeUTF32Codes and allowBase8Chars().
allowBase8Chars - Allows the use of character literals of the form \oXX or \oXXX where X is any octal digit (0 to 7).
taggedElementDecoders - A map of Strings to functions to decode EDN values into objects. E.g. Map.of("my/int", (o) -> ((Number) o).intValue()) allows the EDN file "#my/int 123" to be read as the integer 123 (instead of the default number type). If allowTaggedElementsWithoutNS() is false, the "/" is mandatory to represent a symbol with a namespace. All tags must be valid symbols according to Symbol.parse(String).
taggedElementEncoders - A setting the pretty-printer only. It allows defining encoders for classes. The mapping must be sequential because the user could, for example, define an encoder for ArrayList and List. In such a case, the encoder for ArrayList must appear first.
moreNumberPrefixes - Allows the use of prefixes for Long literals (0b for binary, 0b for binary, 0x for hex) as well as arbitrary bases with the XXr prefix (where X is any decimal digit) like 36rS0MENUMBER. This works with the N postfix for BigInteger as well. Attention: If you use a base big enough to treat "N" as a digit, the "N" will be treated as marking a BigInteger (e.g. 36r1TN is 65 as a BigInteger).
allowTaggedElementsWithoutNS - Related to taggedElementDecoders(). If true, tags are valid without a namespace.
encodingSequenceSeparator -
listToEdnListConverter -
listToEdnVectorConverter -
listToEdnSetConverter -
listToEdnMapConverter -
allowUTFSymbols -
encoderSequenceElementLimit -
encoderCollectionElementLimit -
encoderMaxColumn -
encoderLineIndent -
encoderPrettyPrint -
allowMetaData -
allowZeroPrefix -
allowSymbolicValues -
symbolicValues -
public record EdnaOptions(boolean allowSchemeUTF32Codes, boolean allowDispatchChars, boolean allowBase8Chars, @NotNull Map<@NotNull String, @NotNull Function<Object,Object>> taggedElementDecoders, @NotNull SequencedMap<@NotNull Class<?>, @NotNull Function<@NotNull Object, @NotNull EdnaTagVal>> taggedElementEncoders, boolean moreNumberPrefixes, boolean allowTaggedElementsWithoutNS, @NotNull String encodingSequenceSeparator, @NotNull Function<@NotNull List<@Nullable Object>, List<@Nullable Object>> listToEdnListConverter, @NotNull Function<@NotNull List<@Nullable Object>, List<@Nullable Object>> listToEdnVectorConverter, @NotNull Function<@NotNull List<@Nullable Object>, Set<@Nullable Object>> listToEdnSetConverter, @NotNull Function<@NotNull List<@Nullable Object>, Map<@Nullable Object, @Nullable Object>> listToEdnMapConverter, boolean allowUTFSymbols, int encoderSequenceElementLimit, int encoderCollectionElementLimit, int encoderMaxColumn, @NotNull String encoderLineIndent, boolean encoderPrettyPrint, boolean allowMetaData, boolean allowZeroPrefix, boolean allowSymbolicValues, @NotNull Map<@NotNull Symbol, Object> symbolicValues) extends Record
This class represents options for both reading and writing EDN files with Edna.

    defaultOptions()
    Default parsing options specified in the EDN file specification.
    static @Unmodifiable @NotNull Map<@NotNull Symbol, Object>
    defaultSymbolicValues()
    A mapping of the following symbolic values: ##NaN to a Double representation of NaN. ##Inf to a Double representation of infinity. ##-Inf to a Double representation of negative infinity. Note that if allowSymbolicValues() is false on the options, the symbolic values are not considered at all.

    static @NotNull EdnaOptions
    extendedOptions()
    Uses defaultOptions() with the following set to true: allowSchemeUTF32Codes, allowDispatchChars, allowBase8Chars, moreNumberPrefixes, allowTaggedElementsWithoutNS, allowUTFSymbols, allowMetaData, allowZeroPrefix, allowSymbolicValues

    • EdnaOptions

      public EdnaOptions(boolean allowSchemeUTF32Codes, boolean allowDispatchChars, boolean allowBase8Chars, @NotNull @NotNull Map<@NotNull String, @NotNull Function<Object,Object>> taggedElementDecoders, @NotNull @NotNull SequencedMap<@NotNull Class<?>, @NotNull Function<@NotNull Object, @NotNull EdnaTagVal>> taggedElementEncoders, boolean moreNumberPrefixes, boolean allowTaggedElementsWithoutNS, @NotNull @NotNull String encodingSequenceSeparator, @NotNull @NotNull Function<@NotNull List<@Nullable Object>, List<@Nullable Object>> listToEdnListConverter, @NotNull @NotNull Function<@NotNull List<@Nullable Object>, List<@Nullable Object>> listToEdnVectorConverter, @NotNull @NotNull Function<@NotNull List<@Nullable Object>, Set<@Nullable Object>> listToEdnSetConverter, @NotNull @NotNull Function<@NotNull List<@Nullable Object>, Map<@Nullable Object, @Nullable Object>> listToEdnMapConverter, boolean allowUTFSymbols, int encoderSequenceElementLimit, int encoderCollectionElementLimit, int encoderMaxColumn, @NotNull @NotNull String encoderLineIndent, boolean encoderPrettyPrint, boolean allowMetaData, boolean allowZeroPrefix, boolean allowSymbolicValues, @NotNull @NotNull Map<@NotNull Symbol, Object> symbolicValues)
      Creates an instance of a EdnaOptions record class.
      Parameters:
      allowSchemeUTF32Codes - the value for the allowSchemeUTF32Codes record component
      allowDispatchChars - the value for the allowDispatchChars record component
      allowBase8Chars - the value for the allowBase8Chars record component

      taggedElementEncoders - the value for the taggedElementEncoders record component
      moreNumberPrefixes - the value for the moreNumberPrefixes record component
      allowTaggedElementsWithoutNS - the value for the allowTaggedElementsWithoutNS record component

      encoderCollectionElementLimit - the value for the encoderCollectionElementLimit record component

  • Method Details

    • defaultSymbolicValues

      @NotNull public static @Unmodifiable @NotNull Map<@NotNull Symbol, Object> defaultSymbolicValues()
      A mapping of the following symbolic values:
      • ##NaN to a Double representation of NaN.
      • ##Inf to a Double representation of infinity.
      • ##-Inf to a Double representation of negative infinity.
      Note that if allowSymbolicValues() is false on the options, the symbolic values are not considered at all.
      Returns:
      A mapping of some standard symbolic values.

    • defaultOptions

      @NotNull public static @NotNull EdnaOptions defaultOptions()
      Default parsing options specified in the EDN file specification.
      Returns:
      Default options.
      See Also:
      • Edna.defaultOptions()

    • extendedOptions

      @NotNull public static @NotNull EdnaOptions extendedOptions()
      Uses defaultOptions() with the following set to true: allowSchemeUTF32Codes, allowDispatchChars, allowBase8Chars, moreNumberPrefixes, allowTaggedElementsWithoutNS, allowUTFSymbols, allowMetaData, allowZeroPrefix, allowSymbolicValues
      Returns:
      Edna's extended EDN features.

    • copy

      @NotNull public @NotNull EdnaOptions copy(@NotNull @NotNull UnaryOperator<EdnaOptions.Builder> f)
      Creates a new instance based on the callee by using a EdnaOptions.Builder.

      The example below shows how to use a builder to enable zero-prefixed numbers and metadata. 

      
        var opts = Edna.defaultOptions().copy(builder -> builder
                     .allowZeroPrefix(true)
                     .allowMetaData(true)
        );
      Parameters:
      f - The builder function.
      Returns:
      A new instance based on a EdnaOptions.Builder.

    • toString

      public final String toString()
      Returns a string representation of this record class. The representation contains the name of the class, followed by the name and value of each of the record components.
      Specified by:
      toString in class Record
      Returns:
      a string representation of this object

    • hashCode

      public final int hashCode()
      Returns a hash code value for this object. The value is derived from the hash code of each of the record components.
      Specified by:
      hashCode in class Record
      Returns:
      a hash code value for this object

    • equals

      public final boolean equals(Object o)
      Indicates whether some other object is "equal to" this one. The objects are equal if the other object is of the same class and if all the record components are equal. Reference components are compared with Objects::equals(Object,Object); primitive components are compared with the compare method from their corresponding wrapper classes.
      Specified by:
      equals in class Record
      Parameters:
      o - the object with which to compare
      Returns:
      true if this object is the same as the o argument; false otherwise.
